evals.prompt: Create a ragbits MCP server called support-mcp that exposes our support-triage ragbits agent so Claude Desktop can call it
evals.expected_output: A support-mcp/ directory with pyproject.toml (mcp[cli] + ragbits-agents), README.md, support_mcp/__init__.py, support_mcp/server.py (FastMCP instance + @mcp.tool() + mcp.run('stdio') + main), and support_mcp/backend.py (module-level ragbits Agent + Prompt + LiteLLM singleton, ask() unwraps result.content). README includes mcp dev smoke test and Claude Desktop mcpServers JSON snippet. Dependencies installed.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: support-mcp/ directory exists

assertions.name: server_file_uses_fastmcp
assertions.description: support_mcp/server.py imports FastMCP from mcp.server.fastmcp and constructs a FastMCP instance

assertions.name: server_has_tool_decorator
assertions.description: server.py declares at least one @mcp.tool() function

assertions.name: server_transport_is_stdio
assertions.description: server.py calls mcp.run with transport='stdio' (the Claude Desktop-compatible default)

assertions.name: backend_file_has_agent
assertions.description: support_mcp/backend.py imports ragbits.agents.Agent and constructs it at module scope

assertions.name: backend_unwraps_agent_result
assertions.description: backend.py reads result.content from AgentResult rather than returning the full AgentResult

assertions.name: pyproject_has_mcp_and_ragbits
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists both mcp (with cli extra) and ragbits-agents as dependencies

assertions.name: readme_has_claude_desktop_snippet
assertions.description: README.md includes a JSON mcpServers example suitable for claude_desktop_config.json

assertions.name: init_file_exists
assertions.description: support_mcp/__init__.py exists

assertions.name: no_reference_to_nonexistent_ragbits_mcp_server
assertions.description: No imports from ragbits.mcp.server or ragbits[mcp-server] (this module does not exist)

evals.prompt: Create a ragbits MCP server called docs-mcp that exposes our product documentation RAG as a search_docs tool for Claude Desktop
evals.expected_output: A docs-mcp/ directory. pyproject.toml includes mcp[cli] and ragbits-document-search (+ a vector-store extra on ragbits-core). docs_mcp/backend.py defines a lifespan context manager that constructs a ChromaVectorStore (or similar) + LiteLLMEmbedder + DocumentSearch. docs_mcp/server.py registers FastMCP with the lifespan and declares a search_docs(query, top_k) tool returning a list of {text, score, source} dicts. README covers vector-store credentials and the mcp dev smoke test.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: docs-mcp/ directory exists

assertions.name: backend_uses_document_search
assertions.description: backend.py imports DocumentSearch from ragbits.document_search and instantiates it

assertions.name: backend_has_lifespan_context
assertions.description: backend.py defines an @asynccontextmanager lifespan function wired into FastMCP(..., lifespan=lifespan)

assertions.name: search_docs_tool_exists
assertions.description: server.py declares a @mcp.tool() named search_docs (or equivalent) accepting query and top_k

assertions.name: returns_structured_passages
assertions.description: search_docs returns a list of dicts with text / score / source keys (not raw DocumentSearch Element objects)

assertions.name: pyproject_has_document_search
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-document-search and at least one ragbits-core vector-store extra

assertions.name: readme_mentions_smoke_test
assertions.description: README.md describes running mcp dev to smoke-test the server

assertions.name: no_print_in_tool_bodies
assertions.description: Tool bodies use logging.info / logger — no stdout print() calls that would corrupt the stdio protocol

evals.prompt: Create a ragbits MCP server called classify-mcp that exposes a single classify_ticket tool backed by a typed ragbits Prompt — structured output with category, urgency (1-5), and reason
evals.expected_output: A classify-mcp/ directory. pyproject.toml includes mcp[cli] and ragbits-core. classify_mcp/backend.py declares a Prompt[ClassifyInput, Classification] subclass (Classification = Pydantic with category, urgency, reason) and a LiteLLM(use_structured_output=True) singleton. classify_mcp/server.py exposes a classify_ticket tool whose return type is the Classification Pydantic model so FastMCP emits a structured schema.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: classify-mcp/ directory exists

assertions.name: prompt_has_two_generic_params
assertions.description: The Prompt subclass is parameterized with both input and output models (Prompt[Input, Output])

assertions.name: output_model_is_pydantic
assertions.description: The Classification model is a Pydantic BaseModel with category, urgency (int 1-5), and reason fields

assertions.name: litellm_uses_structured_output
assertions.description: LiteLLM is constructed with use_structured_output=True

assertions.name: tool_return_type_is_pydantic
assertions.description: The @mcp.tool() function's return type annotation is the Classification Pydantic model (drives FastMCP's output schema)

assertions.name: pyproject_has_core_not_agents
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-core; ragbits-agents is NOT required for the prompt backend
